rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LAMONT ALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petiion For Authority To Control Conservatee'S Salary Under Probate Code Section 2601 And For Authority To Pay Conservatee An Allowance For Personal Use Pursuant To Probate Code Section 2421
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance requried. 1. Appears PC 2601 requires this-otherwise-order to have been granted before bringing this petition. Conservator might have brought this petition under PC sec 1830(b). not 2601. 2. Allegation if the conservatorship of estate continues and will continue to meet the requirement of PC sec 2628. 3. Petition needs to seek Letters to be amended to specify these two additional powers being granted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/10/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The copy of the accounting petition received by the examiner does not appear to be a true and correct copy; there is no schedule of property on hand attached to it. (2) The first accounting showed the real property with a value of $1,900,000, but the I&A value was $2,100,000, and this has not been corrected yet. (3) Tangible personal property (the specifically devised Asian ceramics and painting and artwork) were also missing from assets on hand at the end of the first accounting, although they had not apparently been distributed. No receipts were filed for them; they were shown on the Losses schedule in the first accounting for some reason. (4) This accounting shows multiple payments for storage fee and for a safe box rental. If this was to store the tangible personal property appraised at a total of $4,000, this does not appear to be a prudent use of estate funds. (5) Why has the executor not given Notice of Proposed Action to distribute the tangible personal property to the devisees? [Probate Code Section 10520.] (6) The executor advanced $24,000 to the estate, but repayments totaled $25,749.00? (7) What is the $1,980.59 "Counter Credit" on 10/13/21 listed under "Unidentified Deposits"? (8) Who is/are Claiborne Iavoi Associates and for what purpose were they paid $1,050.00 on 3/3/21? (9) Why was Sfrealestate.com paid $3,925.00 on 5/4/21? (10) Attorney costs must be itemized. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/29/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTOINETTE LONGA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although neither the petition caption nor the prayer included the request for attorney fees to be a lien against the real property. This Court permits no more than 5% simple interest on unpaid legal fees secured against real property. [LRSF 14.56.] The petition also did not include the full disclosure required by Probate Code Section 9202(b), including any past confinement of an heir (or beneficiary). The administrator (not "devisee") was appointed on 6/23/21, not 6/29/21, and Letters were issued to him on 6/29/21, not 7/2/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSEMARY ANNE GILBERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress, as the following issues (last updated and e-mailed to counsel on 05/22/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) Re the DE 121 form: i) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. ii) The proof of service does not show the country name as part of the addresses of Stephen Lawson and Elaine Lawson. 2) The petition caption: The IAEA box must be checked. 3) Item 2d of the petition: At least one box must be checked. 4) Item 3d: If the estate value is $0, please explain why the petitioner is opening this probate. 5) Item 3f(2): Are the will and both codicils not self-proving? 6) Item 3h(3) must be checked. 7) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 8) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 9) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. 10) Item 8: i) The petitioner's address must be listed. ii) All devisees and executors (including alternate executors) named in the will and each codicil must be listed. iii) Please provide a family tree with the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents, siblings, and all other known relatives. iv) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts made to identify and locate the decedent's heirs entitled to notice. 11) Item 9: The number of attachment pages must be specified. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/29/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 07/11/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or loged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially with a pleading cover sheet attached. 4) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 5) The petition caption: The box "Authorization to Administer" must be checked. 6) Item 1: The newspaper name must be specified. 7) Item 2b: The proposed executor's name must be specified. 8) Item 3c: The decedent's residence address at the time of her death must be specified. 9) Item 3d: i) Please provide a brief description of the $235,201 personal property. For financial accounts, please state the exact name under which the decedent held title to each account. ii) Is the property the decedent's separate property, her one-half share of community property, or both? Please provide facts in support of the property characterization. 10) Item 3h(3) must be checked. 11) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 12) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 13) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. 14) Item 8: Michael Gordon named in the will as alternate executor must be listed with his current address. Notice must also be given to him. 15) Did the decedent leave a written statement or list of items of personal property other than money described in Article II of the will? If so, please provide a copy.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTHA JANE SHAUL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. No appearance required. Status hearing on close of estate is set for 9/17/2024.
